claim_text: The FY2026 Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies appropriation was enacted on November 12, 2025, as Division D of P.L. 119-37 (H.R. 5371).
pinpoint: Congress.gov bill record H.R. 5371; CRS IN12622
limitations: Bibliographic/structural claim

claim_text: VA's FY2025 appropriations were provided through the Full-Year Continuing Appropriations and Extensions Act, 2025 (Division A of P.L. 119-4), not a regular MilCon-VA act.
limitations: Structural claim; account detail requires CRS Table 8 (D-0014)

claim_text: Cumulative Cost of War Toxic Exposures Fund appropriations FY2022 through FY2026 total $103.459B.
pinpoint: CRS R48968 Tables 1-6; PACT Act TEF authority
derivation: Sum of FY2022-FY2026 TEF appropriations in CRS R48968
limitations: TEF mandatory/discretionary treatment differs by context; no FY2027 enacted data

claim_text: VA FY2025 financial statements received an unmodified audit opinion while the auditor identified two material weaknesses and three significant deficiencies.
pinpoint: FY2025 AFR and OIG audit report language
limitations: Does not establish chronicity without prior-year series

claim_text: The 2025 National Veteran Suicide Prevention Annual Report reports CY2023 suicide data, creating a roughly two-year reporting lag.
limitations: Does not by itself evaluate current program effectiveness

claim_text: The FY2024 VBA supplemental episode concerned benefits obligations and was publicly explained as a reporting/communication problem rather than a failure to pay benefits.
pinpoint: VA statements and Congressional hearing record
limitations: Does not quantify every root cause; specific internal controls require audit access

claim_text: The FY2024 VHA supplemental episode concerned medical-care funding and forecasting under the Fiscal Responsibility Act cap.
pinpoint: Hearing record and supplemental request context
limitations: Depends on hearing and budget narrative; not an independent forensic audit

claim_text: The approximately $5.1B carryover figure remains provenance-labeled and should not be treated as a primary-verified amount in v1.0.
limitations: Legacy/draft provenance caveat

claim_text: FY2026 enacted VA budget authority rose to $445.49B, with growth decomposing primarily through mandatory appropriations rather than discretionary expansion.
derivation: Compare FY2025 enacted total to FY2026 enacted total and mandatory/discretionary components
limitations: Historical-growth passage quarantined by D-0022

claim_text: Toxic Exposures Fund treatment is central to interpreting VA budget growth and must not be blended without basis caveats.
pinpoint: CRS R48968 TEF tables and notes
limitations: TEF construction varies across official presentations

claim_text: The FY2025 OIG severe occupational staffing shortage determination reported 4,434 severe shortages across VHA facilities.
limitations: Agency disputes measure usefulness

claim_text: VA publicly rejected the mandated staffing-shortage instrument as subjective and unreliable, creating a measurement-integrity conflict.
limitations: Press-mediated public quote remains separately limited

claim_text: VA's 2025 suicide report indicates that 61% of veteran suicide decedents had no VHA health care in the prior year.
pinpoint: 2025 National Veteran Suicide Prevention Annual Report
limitations: Does not measure non-VHA interventions or broader determinants

claim_text: Spending-to-outcome claims for veteran suicide prevention must partition VHA users and non-users because VHA reach is structurally incomplete.
limitations: Does not negate value of VHA-based interventions for users

claim_text: GAO's December 2025 EHRM testimony reported unresolved recommendations and cost-estimate concerns while deployments were accelerating.
limitations: Requires current-status check after publication

claim_text: EHR modernization findings in v1.0 are dated and currency-sensitive, including withhold deadlines and recommendation status.
limitations: May be superseded by post-publication records

claim_text: Community-care appointment-timeliness standards and measurement limitations remain material to oversight conclusions.
limitations: Full body not yet deconstructed

claim_text: Community Care Network oversight includes unresolved network-adequacy and claims-data concerns.
limitations: Full bodies not yet deconstructed

claim_text: Community-care growth cannot be interpreted without separating access expansion from measurement adequacy.
pinpoint: Analytical synthesis from oversight and funding records
limitations: Requires full section construction in v1.1

claim_text: VA's FY2025 clean audit opinion must be read alongside the named material weaknesses and significant deficiencies.
pinpoint: FY2025 AFR and OIG audit report
limitations: Does not resolve chronicity without prior-year series

claim_text: The suicide-report findings are bounded by reporting lag and population reach limitations.
limitations: May be updated by Dole Act report or later VA release
